The expansion, with average daily volume above $7 billion, pushes JPMorgan's bank-rail tokenized deposits ahead of public-chain stablecoins for regulated institutions that want near-instant settlement without new counterparty or KYC risk.

title: Crypto firms have spent $189M so far on 2026 US election, report says
blurb: Crypto firms have poured $189M into 2026 US election campaigns so far, according to a new report, already exceeding much of their 2024 midterm spending. The push reflects a coordinated bet on friendlier regulation from Congress and agencies like the SEC and CFTC, as stablecoin and market-structure bills remain unresolved on Capitol Hill.

title: President Trump’s financial disclosures show hundreds of millions in crypto-related income
blurb: President Trump's annual financial disclosure reported over $236M in token-sale proceeds from World Liberty Financial and $65.6M from the sale of his equity in the crypto venture, plus cold-wallet holdings of bitcoin, ether, USDC, and other tokens. The filing lands as lawmakers debate a digital-asset bill, with Democrats pushing an ethics provision to bar the president, vice president, and other officials from certain crypto transactions.

title: DraftKings Launches Proprietary Prediction Markets Exchange With $3.4B Annualized Consumer Volume
blurb: DraftKings launched DKeX, a proprietary prediction markets exchange built on the CFTC license it acquired through Railbird, moving off third-party infrastructure to own the full stack behind its event-contracts business. The platform reported roughly $3.4B in annualized consumer volume and $11.3B in total trading volume for the week ended June 21, and its 30-state sportsbook footprint gives it immediate distribution against Polymarket and Kalshi.

title: Kalshi hit with 14-day restraining order in Michigan, blocking sports prediction markets in state
blurb: A Michigan court granted the state gaming regulator a 14-day restraining order against Kalshi, halting its sports prediction markets in the state pending a hearing. The order deepens the unresolved jurisdictional fight between the CFTC, which regulates Kalshi as a federal exchange, and state regulators arguing the contracts amount to unlicensed sports betting.

title: SEC wins $5.5 million default judgment over alleged fake crypto platform NanoBit
blurb: A federal judge in the Eastern District of New York entered a $5.5M default judgment against NanoBit Limited and five related defendants over a "pig-butchering" scheme that lured 18 investors into a fake crypto trading platform that never executed a single trade. The SEC alleged participants built trust in WhatsApp groups, then wired more than $2M to Hong Kong bank accounts, and all six defendants are now permanently barred from securities offerings.

title: Crypto firms in the UK face stricter FCA rules
blurb: The FCA published its final rulebook bringing UK crypto firms under financial-services regulation, requiring trading platforms, custodians, and stablecoin issuers to obtain authorisation, hold capital, pass stress tests, and follow market-integrity rules on insider trading and manipulation. The regime takes effect in October 2027 and includes tailored stablecoin standards and simplified capital requirements, narrowing the gap between crypto firms and the banks and payment providers they increasingly compete with.

title: Spot bitcoin ETFs record worst month since debut, shedding $4.5 billion in June
blurb: U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs shed $4.5 billion in net outflows in June, their worst month since launching in January 2024 and 29% above the prior record set in February 2025. Analysts attribute the exodus to a macro rotation out of higher-volatility assets and capital pulled into SpaceX's $75 billion IPO, with total ETF net assets falling to roughly $70.9 billion from peaks above $110 billion.

title: CFTC Expands Polymarket Probe to Staged Trades and Fake Wins
blurb: The CFTC expanded its Polymarket investigation to cover staged trades and fabricated winning bets, Bloomberg reported, widening a probe that began as an advertising-disclosure inquiry into the platform's paid-influencer scheme. The escalation follows a bipartisan Senate letter citing a WSJ finding that paid creators filmed roughly $900,000 in fake wins on dummy sites, and it revives jurisdictional questions about whether the CFTC can police event-contract platforms as a gaming regulator.

title: SEC opens ETF rule review following crypto fund surge, prediction markets push
blurb: The SEC opened a 60-day public comment period on how to regulate "novel ETFs," including crypto funds and a proposed class tied to prediction markets, after Chair Paul Atkins greenlit dozens of altcoin ETFs spanning SOL to DOGE since April 2025. TD Cowen expects the review to produce rule changes as soon as 2027 that could standardize listings for crypto, event-contract, and single-stock funds.

title: Jefferies warns of crypto market volatility as Clarity Act faces Senate test
blurb: Jefferies warned that the Clarity Act faces a difficult Senate path, with roughly 20 legislative days before the August recess to reconcile competing versions, clear procedural votes, and align with the House bill. Passage would give banks, asset managers, and exchanges the regulatory framework to expand tokenization, custody, and staking, while delays prolong uncertainty and drive volatility across crypto tokens and blockchain-linked equities.

title: SEC giving novel ETFs a rethink as it opens comment period on overhauling U.S. rules
blurb: The SEC opened a 60-day comment period on overhauling its ETF listing rules, signaling it may extend the generic-listing standards that let funds reach markets without individual exemptive relief to novel assets like crypto, event contracts, and single-stock strategies. Chairman Paul Atkins framed the review as a response to an ETF market that grew from $4T in 2019 to $12T in 2025, and analysts read it as groundwork for approving a far broader universe of tradable funds.

title: New Crypto Bill Draft Expected Soon
blurb: Senate Republicans plan to release an updated draft of their industry-backed crypto market-structure bill soon, revised to address members' concerns over ethics provisions. The bill would establish a federal framework for regulating digital assets, clarifying oversight splits between the SEC and CFTC that fintech and crypto firms have sought for years.

title: OKX AI unveils marketplace for agents to find work and get paid in stablecoins
blurb: OKX launched OKX AI, a beta marketplace where autonomous agents post tasks, list services, and settle payments in USDT or USDG stablecoins through escrow contracts or instant pay-per-call transactions. Each agent carries a persistent shared identity that accrues reputation across jobs, and the launch is backed by AWS, the Ethereum and Solana Foundations, CertiK, and StraitsX as OKX builds payment rails for agent-to-agent commerce.

title: SumUp launches consumer account paying users to shop small
blurb: SumUp launched a consumer bank account offering 5% cashback on purchases within its merchant network, extending the small-business payments company into personal banking. The two-sided play waives transaction fees for merchants accepting SumUp consumer users, pitting SumUp against Revolut, Monzo, and N26 in a crowded European neobank market that Wise and Klarna have also entered.

title: Scaling Network Analysis for Fraud Prevention with BigQuery Graph
blurb: Curve, the UK financial super-app, moved its fraud-detection network analysis to BigQuery Graph, using native GQL to trace the multi-hop links between accounts that share devices, funding cards, or contact details. Running graph queries directly inside its existing BigQuery warehouse let Curve surface organized fraud rings without migrating to a dedicated graph database, avoiding costly self-joins and producing significant transaction savings.

title: Securitize Set to Go Public Thursday
blurb: Securitize will begin trading on the NYSE this Thursday after investors approved its SPAC merger, making it one of the first publicly traded pure-play tokenization companies. The listing gives public-market investors direct exposure to the real-world-asset tokenization thesis, and sets an early valuation benchmark for infrastructure firms building settlement rails for on-chain funds and securities.

title: FCA reveals ‘landmark’ crypto rules
blurb: The FCA published its landmark crypto regulatory framework, requiring trading platforms, custodians, intermediaries, and stablecoin issuers to obtain authorisation and meet capital, stress-testing, and market-integrity standards when the rules take effect in October 2027. Stablecoin oversight, developed jointly with the Bank of England, includes a path from FCA supervision to joint regulation once HM Treasury deems an issuer systemic, giving UK crypto firms a clearer runway than the current promotions-and-AML-only regime.

title: Sharplink buys 10,000 ETH, repurchases 2.13 million SBET shares in latest buyback
blurb: SharpLink bought 10,000 ETH at an average $1,611 per token, lifting its treasury to 886,725 ETH and marking its first disclosed ether purchase in eight months, funded partly by a $75M registered direct offering. The Miami company also repurchased 2.13 million SBET shares under a buyback that has now retired 4.07 million shares since August 2025, tying its capital allocation to growing ETH per share.

title: Morgan Stanley cut its riskiest reconciliation job in half — by making its agents less autonomous
blurb: Morgan Stanley deployed an agentic system called FIXR to handle P&L reconciliation across its Finance, Risk, Operations, and Trade Capture systems, cutting the time to clear daily "breaks" from up to six hours per book to two or three and saving roughly 1,500 controller hours a week. The gains came from keeping humans tightly in the loop, with agents codifying repeated controller decisions into durable rules rather than acting autonomously.

title: Self-exiled Chinese billionaire sentenced to 30 years for fraud involving crypto
blurb: A New York federal court sentenced self-exiled Chinese billionaire Guo Wengui to 30 years in prison for orchestrating a fraud conspiracy that prosecutors valued at more than $1 billion, partly run through cryptocurrency schemes. The case underscores how crypto fundraising remains a recurring vehicle for large-scale investor fraud, keeping pressure on the SEC and DOJ to police token-linked offerings aimed at retail backers.

title: Why OpenUSD's 'real threat' that tanked Circle stock still faces a steep uphill battle for adoption
blurb: Circle shares fell 16% after the launch of Open Standard, a 140-company consortium backed by Stripe, Coinbase, Visa, Mastercard, and BlackRock, whose Open USD stablecoin would share reserve yield with partners rather than keep it like USDC. Analysts call the selloff an overreaction, noting that Paxos' consortium-backed USDG has reached only $3B in supply and that OUSD still faces open questions on structure, incentives, and distribution.

title: Shanghai court sentences five to prison over $29 million crypto forex scheme
blurb: A Shanghai court sentenced five people to prison for running a $29M scheme that used cryptocurrency to facilitate illegal foreign-exchange transfers, part of a case Chinese authorities opened in July 2024 after flagging unusual transactions. The convictions reinforce Beijing's treatment of crypto as a channel for evading capital controls, keeping enforcement pressure high on any firm routing cross-border value through digital assets.

title: Dish files for bankruptcy, but not shutting down
blurb: EchoStar-owned Dish filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy after failing to repay $2 billion in debt due July 1st, citing insufficient liquidity from stalled 5G spectrum sales to AT&T and SpaceX. Dish TV, Sling TV, Boost Mobile, and Gen Mobile keep operating through the process, with the company targeting an exit from Chapter 11 by the end of Q3 2026.

title: Uber-Backed Bike Firm Lime, Investors Raise $174 Million in IPO
blurb: Lime, the Uber-backed micromobility company formally known as Neutron Holdings, raised $174 million in its US IPO, pricing at $25 per share, the midpoint of its marketed range. The listing marks one of the first venture-backed consumer transport debuts of the year and gives Uber, a major shareholder, a public benchmark for the scooter and bike-share assets it has spun out rather than operated directly.

title: Regulator targets Apple & Google over steering, NFC tech
blurb: The UK's Competition and Markets Authority is consulting on conduct requirements that would force Apple and Google to let developers steer users to off-platform payment options and access iOS NFC hardware directly. The changes would let UK fintechs run contactless card payments inside their own apps and bypass mandatory app-store fees, which the CMA expects would fall below current platform charges.

title: The Lowest-Logical-Fare Problem
blurb: Expense management software can automate a travel policy but not the judgment behind it, because the cheapest bookable option often isn't the cheapest actual outcome once you count connections, drive times, and a traveler's wasted hours. The author calls the real target the "lowest logical fare," a per-trip judgment call that no hard-coded rule survives. The moment you write the rule down, reality produces the exception.

title: Apple Pay now lets you pay using American Express points
blurb: American Express Membership Rewards points can now be redeemed directly inside the Apple Pay checkout flow, letting cardholders apply points to cover all or part of any online or in-app purchase without leaving the payment screen. Apple retains no transaction data from these redemptions, and the native integration removes the separate redemption steps that previously pushed points-paying outside the primary checkout.

title: In Treasury Management, to the Victor go the Spoils
blurb: Legacy banks face a worsening retention problem with commercial clients as a younger generation of CFOs, comfortable with modern software, demands real-time visibility, instant issuance, and clean reconciliation from their treasury management providers. The author argues that tech firms have raised the baseline on these features, and banks that leaned on decades-old cores now must match digital-first competitors or watch business deposits move elsewhere.

title: EquiLibre secures Series A at €438 million valutation to scale AI trading agents handling billions daily
blurb: EquiLibre Technologies, a Prague-based lab applying reinforcement learning to live trading, closed a Series A at a valuation above €438M ($500M), led by Creandum in what is reportedly the firm's largest check to date. Most of the capital will fund one of the region's largest compute clusters, backing agents the company says trade billions of dollars daily and have never posted a losing month.

title: NatWest completes £2.7bn swoop for Evelyn Partners
blurb: NatWest Group completed its £2.7bn acquisition of wealth manager Evelyn Partners, exiting Permira and Warburg Pincus and creating what the firms call the UK's leading private banking and wealth management business with £127bn in combined assets. NatWest expects roughly £100m in annual run-rate cost synergies against £150m in costs to achieve, deepening big-bank push into fee-based wealth revenue.

title: Don't See Slowdown in Institutional Client Interest: Markova
blurb: Fireblocks policy director Dea Markova told Bloomberg that institutional client interest in digital assets shows no signs of slowing, even as the market awaits an updated draft of the Clarity Act expected soon. The forthcoming market-structure text would clarify which tokens fall under SEC versus CFTC jurisdiction, a split that has kept many banks and custodians on the sidelines of the crypto business.
